_comment: California State Teachers' Retirement System (CalSTRS) — Defined Benefit Program. Consumer-agnostic data only. No Meridian-specific references.
administeredBy: CalSTRS — governed by the Teachers' Retirement Board (12 members)

totalMembers.note: ~990K total active, retired, and beneficiaries as of 2025. Largest educator-only pension fund in the world.

populations: Certain county office of education employees
excluded: University of California employees (covered by UCRP)

socialSecurity.note: CalSTRS members do not pay into or receive Social Security from CalSTRS-covered service. California educators are exempt from Social Security under Section 218 agreements. The Social Security Fairness Act (H.R. 82, signed Jan 5 2025) eliminated WEP and GPO for SS benefits payable after Dec 2023 — CalSTRS retirement benefits themselves are unaffected.

programs: Defined Benefit (DB) Program — primary pension, modeled here
Defined Benefit Supplement (DBS) Program — separate account for excess service earnings
Cash Balance Benefit Program — for part-time/adjunct educators, DC-style
programStructure.note: This file models the DB Program only. DBS and Cash Balance are separate structures.

tier_2pct_at_60.eligibility: First hired to perform CalSTRS-creditable service on or before December 31, 2012

formula.structure: Age Factor x Years of Service Credit x Final Compensation

minimum.note: 1.1% age factor at age 50 — minimum retirement age with 30+ years of service

maximum.note: 2.4% age factor at age 63 or older

ageFactor.note: Age factor increases gradually by quarter-year of age. Full breakpoint table in CalSTRS 'The Benefit of a Lifetime' publication.

careerFactor.requirement: 30 or more years of service credit
careerFactor.note: Add 0.2% to age factor for members with 30+ years of service credit. Maximum age factor with career factor is 2.4%.

method_post1996.description: Average annual compensation earnable over highest 36 consecutive months
method_post1996.appliesTo: Members hired on or after January 1, 1997

method_pre1997.description: Average annual compensation earnable over highest 12 consecutive months

compensationCap.note: No PEPRA pensionable compensation cap for Classic (2% at 60) members. IRS 415(b) limits apply.

normalRetirement.note: Age 60 with 5 years of service credit

earlyRetirement.note: Unreduced benefit at age 50 with 30+ years of service
Reduced benefit at age 55 with 5 years; also available through concurrent retirement with certain California public retirement systems

vesting.note: 5 years of service credit required for vested benefit

memberContribution.note: 10.25% of creditable earnings. Rate has been fixed at 10.25% since July 1, 2016 under the CalSTRS Funding Plan.

tier_2pct_at_62.eligibility: First hired to perform CalSTRS-creditable service on or after January 1, 2013 (PEPRA)

formula.structure: Age Factor x Years of Service Credit x Final Compensation

minimum.note: 1.16% age factor at age 55 — minimum retirement age for PEPRA members

maximum.note: 2.4% age factor at age 65 or older

ageFactor.note: Age factor increases gradually by quarter-year of age. Full breakpoint table in CalSTRS 'The Benefit of a Lifetime' publication.

careerFactor.note: PEPRA members are NOT eligible for the 0.2% career factor enhancement.

method.description: Average annual compensation earnable over highest 36 consecutive months. No 12-month option.
method.appliesTo: All CalSTRS 2% at 62 members regardless of hire date

compensationCap.note: Annual creditable compensation cap for PEPRA members, indexed to CPI annually. Members cannot earn service credit or contributions on earnings above this cap.

normalRetirement.note: Age 62 with 5 years of service credit

earlyRetirement.note: Reduced benefit available at age 55 with 5 years of service

retirementEligibility.note: PEPRA members cannot retire as early as age 50 — minimum is age 55.

vesting.note: 5 years of service credit required for vested benefit. Same as Classic tier.

memberContribution.note: 10.205% of creditable earnings for FY 2025-26. Rate is set at 50% of the normal cost of benefits, adjusted annually based on actuarial valuation. Rate may change year to year.
memberContribution.rateSettingAuthority: Teachers' Retirement Board, per Education Code — 50% of normal cost

employerContributions.note: 19.10% of creditable earnings to Teachers' Retirement Fund for FY 2025-26. Additional 8.25% to DBS. Board has authority to adjust employer rate up or down up to 1% per year, maximum 20.25%.

stateContributions.note: State of California contributes 2.5% of prior-year covered payroll to the Supplemental Benefit Maintenance Account (SBMA) annually from the General Fund.
stateContributions.source: Education Code Section 22954; CalSTRS Funding Plan

cola.note: CalSTRS applies a 2% SIMPLE (non-compounding) annual benefit improvement factor to all DB Program retirees. This is NOT compounding — it adds 2% of the original (initial) benefit each year, not 2% of the current benefit. This is a key distinction from CalPERS (compounding) and from standard CPI-linked COLAs.
cola.effectiveDate: September 1 each year following first anniversary of effective retirement date
cola.measurementBasis: Fixed 2% of original base allowance — not CPI-linked

sbma.name: Supplemental Benefit Maintenance Account
sbma.note: When inflation erodes purchasing power of a member's benefit below 85% of the initial benefit, CalSTRS pays quarterly supplemental payments from the SBMA to restore purchasing power to the 85% floor. If SBMA funds are insufficient to maintain 85%, the floor may be reduced to no lower than 80%. Board adjusts the floor between 80-85% based on actuarial sufficiency projections.
sbma.inflationMeasure: California CPI-U (Department of Finance, June figure for each fiscal year)
sbma.fundingSufficiency: Projected solvent through 2089 at 85% protection level (68% probability per stochastic model)
sbma.fundingSource: State General Fund contributions (2.5% of payroll) + School Lands Revenue + investment returns credited at assumed DB rate (7%)
sbma.eligibilityDetermination: Automated annual review; members receive confirmation letter if eligible
sbma.authority: Education Code Sections 22216, 22954, 22954.1, 24415, 24415.5, 24416; California Code of Regulations Section 26000

schoolLandsRevenue.note: School Lands Revenue (from state school land grants) provides purchasing power restoration from 0% to 80%. SBMA covers from 80% to 85%. Together they provide the full 85% floor.

serviceCredit.note: Maximum 1.0 year of service credit earned per school year from CalSTRS-covered service. Part-time service earns proportional credit.
purchaseOptions: Other California public retirement system service (subject to reciprocity)

coverageA.name: Coverage A — Career Factor / Disability Allowance
coverageA.note: Automatic basic disability coverage. Benefit based on years of service credit with minimum of 1.4% of final compensation for service-connected disability.

coverageB.name: Coverage B — Enhanced Disability
coverageB.note: Optional enhanced disability coverage. Higher benefit formula. Member elects at hire.

preRetirementDeath.note: One-time death benefit amounts effective July 1, 2025. Coverage A is automatic; Coverage B is optional elected coverage.

postRetirementDeath.note: Same $7,288 death benefit after retirement under both Coverage A and Coverage B.

options: Option 1 — Lump sum refund to beneficiary of remaining contributions
Option 2 — 100% of monthly benefit continues to beneficiary for life
Option 3 — 50% of monthly benefit continues to beneficiary for life
Compound Option — multiple beneficiaries with different option elections
optionBeneficiaries.note: Electing a survivor option reduces the monthly retirement benefit actuarially.

returnToWork.note: Retired members who return to CalSTRS-covered work are limited in annual earnings. Limit indexed annually. Working beyond the limit may affect retirement benefit. Exceptions apply for critical shortage positions.

irc415bLimits.note: IRS 415(b) limits the dollar amount CalSTRS can pay for a retirement benefit; limits vary by age at retirement and are indexed annually.

systems: Other California county retirement systems (1937 Act)
reciprocity.note: Reciprocity allows members who move between eligible systems to have service in both systems recognized for retirement eligibility. Final compensation uses the higher-paying system. Members must apply for reciprocity — it is not automatic.

fundingStatus.note: Approximate funded ratio as of June 30, 2025. CalSTRS is on a legislated path to full funding by 2046 under the CalSTRS Funding Plan (AB 1469, 2014). Funded ratio has improved from ~67% in 2014.

researchGaps: Full age factor tables by quarter-year of age (published in CalSTRS PDF at calstrs.com/the-age-factor — site blocks automated access)
DBS interest credit rate history and current rate
Cash Balance Benefit Program contribution rates and current interest credit rate

reducedBenefitElection._comment: Alternate early retirement program for 2% at 60 members only. NOT available to 2% at 62 (PEPRA) members.
reducedBenefitElection.how_it_works: Member receives 50% of the monthly benefit calculated as if they were age 60. The reduced benefit continues for the same number of months after age 60 that the member received benefits before age 60. After that period, the full normal retirement benefit begins.
reducedBenefitElection.benefit_adjustments: Annual benefit adjustments accrue at age 60 but are not paid until the member starts receiving the full normal retirement benefit.

example._note: Reduced benefit = 50% of benefit calculated at age 60. Full benefit resumes after equal time past age 60.

definedBenefitSupplement._comment: Hybrid cash balance supplement to the DB plan. All CalSTRS DB members have a DBS account.
definedBenefitSupplement.description: The Defined Benefit Supplement (DBS) is a supplemental cash balance account that accrues alongside the primary DB benefit. Funded by employee contributions on compensation above the creditable compensation limit (for 2% at 62 members) and employer contributions.
contribution_sources: Employee contributions on excess compensation (above creditable comp cap for PEPRA members)
Employer DBS contributions (separate from DB employer rate)

interest_credit_rate.description: Interest credited annually at a rate determined by the CalSTRS board, based on investment returns.

payout_options: Rollover to CalSTRS Pension2 or other qualified plan
definedBenefitSupplement._note_below_minimum: Accounts under $3,500 at retirement are paid as lump sum only.
definedBenefitSupplement._source: CalSTRS Member Handbook 2025; CalSTRS Your Retirement Guide 2025

cashBalanceBenefitProgram._comment: Alternative retirement plan for part-time, substitute, and temporary educators. Separate from the DB plan.
cashBalanceBenefitProgram.description: The Cash Balance Benefit Program (CBBP) is a defined benefit plan with individual accounts for part-time educators. Benefits are based on account balance (contributions + interest credits), not a formula.
cashBalanceBenefitProgram.eligibility: Part-time, substitute, and temporary employees performing CalSTRS-creditable service who are not already DB members. Employer must offer CBBP as an alternative.
key_features: Individual account with guaranteed minimum interest credit
No age factor or service credit formula — benefit based on account balance
Can elect DB membership at any time (contributions transfer, CBBP account closes)
Payout: lump sum, annuity, or rollover at separation
cashBalanceBenefitProgram.interaction_with_db: If a CBBP member later becomes a full-time DB member, their CBBP account closes and all creditable service transfers to the DB plan.
